plan.name: (Sample) SF Bay Area Mixed Mesh
plan.description: 6-node mesh network across the San Francisco Bay Area with varying power levels, antenna heights, and devices. Covers hilly urban terrain (Twin Peaks, Marin Headlands), over-water propagation (Bay crossings), and mixed suburban/urban environments.

nodes.name: Twin Peaks Relay
nodes.notes: High-power solar relay on Twin Peaks (282m elevation). Best vantage point in SF with 360-degree line of sight across the city and Bay. Primary backbone node running at maximum power.

nodes.name: Ferry Building Gateway
nodes.notes: Waterfront node at the Embarcadero. Moderate power, low antenna height. Tests urban downtown propagation and over-water path to Treasure Island.

nodes.name: Marin Headlands Hilltop
nodes.notes: Solar-powered hilltop relay in the Marin Headlands (240m elevation), north of Golden Gate Bridge. High power for long-range Bay coverage. Tests cross-water propagation to SF and East Bay.

nodes.name: Treasure Island Node
nodes.notes: Low-power portable node on Treasure Island in the middle of the Bay. Tests over-water propagation to both SF and Oakland. Minimal antenna height simulates handheld use.

nodes.name: Oakland Hills Repeater
nodes.notes: Rooftop repeater in the Oakland Hills (480m elevation). Tall antenna on elevated terrain gives excellent Bay-wide coverage. Tests East Bay to SF link across water and the Bay Bridge corridor.

nodes.name: Mission District Portable
nodes.notes: Handheld T-Echo in the Mission District at street level. Lowest power node (14 dBm) simulating a pedestrian user. Tests worst-case urban canyon reception from the Twin Peaks relay above.
